name: Samoa
avoid: November–April is wetter and overlaps the South Pacific cyclone season, so swimming conditions, road plans, and ferry schedules are less predictable.
Sunday is the quietest day in Samoa: many shops close, buses thin out, and villages expect a more respectful, low-key pace.
Dry-season school holidays and festival weeks can book out the best beach fales early, especially around Lalomanu and Manase.
stopoverNote: For trips under 5 days, stay on Upolu for Apia plus one south-coast beach base; adding Savai'i makes the pace ferry-heavy.

cities.name: Apia
cities.notes: Apia is the best practical base for museums, markets, harbour sunsets, and north-coast day trips before you head to Samoa's beach and ferry legs.

cities.name: Lalomanu
cities.notes: Lalomanu is Samoa's classic east-Upolu beach stretch for fale stays, To Sua access, and slower lagoon time on the island's drier-season south coast.

cities.name: Savai'i
cities.notes: Savai'i rewards the extra ferry with lava fields, blowholes, village beaches, and a more spacious, less-developed island rhythm than Upolu.

itinerary.sim: Vodafone Samoa is the easiest all-round prepaid option around Apia and Upolu, while Digicel Samoa is the usual backup; download offline maps before crossing to Savai'i.

Apia.name: Apia
Apia.note: Use Apia as the practical landing base for culture, food, and light day trips before shifting to Samoa's beach and ferry segments.

days.theme: Museum Morning, Market Stops and a Harbour-Side First Day

activities.name: Robert Louis Stevenson Museum
activities.tip: Adult entry is commonly SAT20 with a guided house-and-gardens tour included; aim for a weekday morning for the fullest access.

activities.name: Palolo Deep Marine Reserve snorkel
activities.tip: Adult entry is typically around SAT5; reef shoes help on the ladder and coral entry.

days.theme: North-Coast Swim Stops and Scenic Upolu Lookouts

activities.name: Piula Cave Pool
activities.tip: Expect SAT5 per person plus a SAT10 vehicle fee, or around SAT15 pp on some recent visits; carry cash and modest swimwear.

hotels.name: Sheraton Samoa Aggie Grey's Hotel & Bungalows

Lalomanu.name: Lalomanu
Lalomanu.note: Lalomanu works best as Samoa's slow-beach pause: one scenic transfer day anchored by To Sua, then an easy lagoon-and-fale reset.

days.theme: South-Coast Drive, To Sua Swim and a Classic Beach-Fale Check-In

activities.name: To Sua Ocean Trench
activities.tip: Adult entry is usually SAT15; the ladder is steep, and the site is typically open Monday-Saturday with shorter Sunday hours.

activities.name: Snorkel or kayak hire from a beach fale

days.theme: Sunrise Lagoon Time and a Slower East-Upolu Beach Day

Savai'i.name: Savai'i
Savai'i.note: Give Savai'i at least two nights so the ferry effort pays off with one volcanic west-coast day and one slower beach-or-waterfall day.

days.theme: Lady Samoa Crossing, Lava Fields and the Blowholes

activities.name: Lady Samoa ferry from Mulifanua to Salelologa
activities.tip: Walk-on adult fares are typically SAT10 one way; taking a rental car usually costs about SAT80–95 extra per vehicle, so reserve ahead in peak season.

activities.name: Saleaula Lava Fields
activities.tip: Adult entry is commonly SAT10 and often includes a short village-led explanation of the lava flow and church ruins.

activities.name: Alofaaga Blowholes
activities.tip: Village-run entry is usually SAT5–10 cash; sea spray is strongest on a rougher swell, but keep well back from the edge.

days.theme: North-Coast Lagoon Time and a Waterfall Detour

activities.name: Afu Aau Waterfall
activities.tip: Most visits involve a small village fee, commonly around SAT5–10 per adult, and the pool is best before heavier afternoon cloud.

activities.name: Satoalepai Wetlands and Giant Clam Sanctuary
activities.tip: Budget a small village cash fee, usually around SAT5–10, for clam-pond access and guiding.

connections.method: Airport shuttle, local bus from the main road, or a pre-booked hotel transfer into central Apia.
South-coast bus or rental car via Lufilufi and the Aleipata district; the drive is scenic but slower than it looks on the map.
Road transfer to Mulifanua Wharf followed by the Lady Samoa ferry to Salelologa, then continue by bus or rental car to your beach stay.
Return across Upolu by rental car or bus to Mulifanua Wharf, then take the Lady Samoa ferry to Salelologa and continue north or west on Savai'i.
Reverse the island hop: Salelologa to Mulifanua on the Lady Samoa ferry, then road transfer back to Apia or Faleolo.
Ferry from Salelologa to Mulifanua and a short onward road transfer to Faleolo for departure.

extras: Carry small tala cash for village-run attractions, bus fares, and ferry kiosks because cards are far less reliable outside Apia.
Rental cars are the easiest way to stitch together To Sua, Lalomanu, and Savai'i without losing daylight, but you need to validate your foreign licence locally first.
Beach fales often bundle breakfast and dinner, so confirm meal plans before double-booking restaurant reservations on the south coast or in Manase.
Reef shoes, a dry bag, and modest cover-ups are especially useful because many Samoa swim spots use ladders, rocky entries, and village-managed changing areas.
